key_insight: The paper replaces global score estimation with local heat-ball mean-value identities for the Fokker–Planck equation, enabling exact score, density, and entropy representations even for nonlinear, state-dependent diffusion.
engineering_relevance: This gives score-based samplers a more local and potentially more accurate estimator in low-density regions, with an exact high-dimensional Monte Carlo sampler that is practical for large-scale generative modeling.
method: Time-change the Fokker–Planck PDE into a standard inhomogeneous heat equation.
Extend heat-ball monotonicity to derive local mean-value formulas for score, density, log-density, and entropy density.
Introduce a factored κ-measure sampler with unit weights and χ²₂ radial concentration for efficient high-dimensional estimation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on 2D structured data, 256-dimensional MNIST, and a sampler study that confirms the predicted concentration behavior.
watch_item: The framework assumes an explicit dimension-dependent drift budget for local well-posedness, so applicability may depend on the drift strength and numerical stability of the heat-ball integrals.
paper_title: Local Fokker--Planck Geometry for Score Estimation: Heat-Ball Mean-Value Representations and Exact High-Dimensional Sampling
